steps.title: Read off x directly
steps.content: The first equation is $x = 7$. This is already solved — no algebra needed. The solution must have $x = 7$.

steps.title: Substitute into the second equation
steps.content:
The second equation is $y = x - 11$. Plug in $x = 7$:
$$y = 7 - 11 = -4$$
The solution is $(7, -4)$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• $\left(0,0 ight)$: $x = 0 e 7$, so this fails the first equation immediately. ✗
  • $\left(7,-4 ight)$: $x = 7$ ✓ and $y = 7 - 11 = -4$ ✓. Both equations satisfied. ✓
  • $\left(7,-11 ight)$: $x = 7$ ✓, but $y = 7 - 11 = -4 e -11$. Fails the second equation. ✗
  • $\left(7,11 ight)$: $x = 7$ ✓, but $y = 7 - 11 = -4 e 11$. The sign is wrong. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
